text: In the product of 64.5 × 0.02, what is the place value of the digit '9'?
Round off '5,184,523' to the nearest ten thousand.
What is the difference between the values of the digit '7' in '472,081' and '306,071'?
In 2010, tourists from Europe numbered 3,842,000 and from Asia 24,573,000. What was the total number of tourists rounded to the nearest million?
If 562,340 > Y > 559,900, which is a possible value of Y?
What is the sum of the largest 5-digit even number and the smallest 3-digit odd number?
Which of the following has its value nearest to 7.3?
Round off the number '48,756,391' to the nearest ten thousand.
Mrs Lam has $738,542 in her savings account. Rewrite the amount to the nearest thousand dollars.
What is the difference between the values of the digits '6' and '3' in the number '5,640,308'?
Which of the following fractions is a possible answer for □ where 7/9 > □ > 3/4?
Arrange the following fractions in ascending order: 2 5/8, 2 3/4, 2 7/12, 2 1/2
A 4 1/2 L jug of juice is shared equally among 6 glasses. How much juice is in each glass?
Kevin has 5 5/6 L of water. He pours it into bottles of 7/9 L each. How much water is left over?
A 3 1/5 L bowl of soup was shared. Alice drank 4/5 L, Ben drank 11/15 L, and Clara drank 7/9 L. Who drank the least?
When calculating 4.5 × 0.2, Sam calculated 45 × 2 instead. His answer is how many times the correct answer?
Which of the following expressions is most suitable for estimating the value of 8.253 + 3.748 − 5.119?
Mrs Wong bakes a loaf of bread using 0.45 kg of flour and 0.08 kg of yeast. How much flour is needed to bake 8 such loaves?
Which of the following has the largest value?
Which of the following expressions has a different answer from the others?
Bottle X has a capacity of 2.4 L and 0.65 L of water is needed to fill it. Bottle Y contains 1.5 L of water. What is the difference in the amount of water in Bottle X and Bottle Y?
The side length of a square tile is 0.45 m. What is the area of the tile? (Give the answer correct to the nearest hundredth.)
Which of the following sets of numbers has 12 as their H.C.F.?
List all the prime numbers from 20 to 40 inclusive.
12 is a common multiple of ___.
What is the sum of the prime numbers from 20 to 40?
List out all the prime numbers from 90 to 110.
List all the prime numbers from 55 to 80 inclusive.
Which of the following numbers is a prime number?
How many common factors do 24 and 36 have?
Jack writes three numbers: 12, 16 and 18. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: A. 3 is a common factor of the three numbers
B. The L.C.M. of the three numbers is 36
C. The H.C.F. of the three numbers is 2
D. 4 is a common factor of 12, 16 and 18

text: Which of the following stands for 'multiply m by 5 and then subtract 9'?
Michael has 88 stamps. Michael has 12 more stamps than Peter. If Peter has p stamps, which equation is correct?
There are 200 pupils in Primary 4. N of them are girls. One third of the boys are wearing glasses. How many boys are wearing glasses?
Amy had 6 boxes of pencils. In each box there were Z blue pencils and 4 red pencils. Altogether she had 54 pencils. Which equation finds the number of blue pencils in each box?
Solve the equation: 45v = 9
The base and height of a triangle are s cm and 9 cm respectively. Its area is 63 cm². Set an equation to find the base of the triangle.
The cost of a book was $25. Danny bought 7 books. He had $45 left over. How much money did Danny have at first? (Use equation to solve the problem.)
Sally has W notebooks that cost $8 each and 6 notebooks that cost $12 each. Write an algebraic expression to show the total cost of all the notebooks.
The base and height of a triangle are Q cm and 10 cm respectively. Write an algebraic expression to show the area of the triangle.
Mr Lee bought a watch online. The delivery fee was $45. The total cost of the watch and delivery fee was $298. What was the cost of the watch? (Use equation to solve the problem.)
Mrs Tam had 75 oranges. After giving some oranges to her neighbours, there were 28 oranges left. How many oranges did Mrs Tam give away? (Use equation.)
Mangoes are sold at $28.50 per kilogram. Find the cost of 3.4 kg of mangoes.
Sam weighs 48 3/4 kg. He is 3 1/8 kg lighter than his brother. What is his brother's weight?
Emily weighs 38.4 kg. Her mother's weight is 1.5 times that of Emily. What is her mother's weight?
Mrs Wong makes a curtain with 2 1/4 m of fabric. If one metre of fabric costs $60, how much does she need to pay for making 2 such curtains?
David has 0.4 times as many stamps as Kevin. If Kevin has 180 stamps, how many stamps does David have?
Betty walks from her house to school and back each day. She walks 2 3/5 km in total per day. How far is her school from her house?
A machine can produce a toy car in 1 1/2 minutes. How many toy cars can be made in 1 hour?
Susan had 240 flowers. 1/6 of the flowers were white and 5/8 were red. The remaining flowers were yellow. How many yellow flowers did Susan have?
A 1/4 L bottle of cooking oil costs $6 3/4. How many such bottles can be bought with $54?
There are 168 animals in a farm. 3/8 of the animals are cows and 1/4 of them are pigs. Find the total number of cows and pigs.
Jack runs around a rectangular park 5 times. If he runs 4 1/2 km in total, what is the perimeter of the park?
A bag of rice costs $12.50 and a bag of flour costs $8.80. What will be the change if Tom pays $100 for 3 bags of rice and 4 bags of flour?
A container 2/3 full holds 2 L of water. A bucket needs 9 L of water. How many such full containers of water are needed to fill the bucket?
2/3 of a dozen oranges weigh 480 g. Find the weight of each orange.
A wire 8 3/4 m long is cut into 7 equal parts. Find the length of each part.
A rectangle is made up of 3 identical squares. If the area of each square is 25 cm², find the perimeter of the rectangle.
The side of an equilateral triangle is 8.35 cm. What is its perimeter?
The height of a trapezium is 12 cm and its area is 120 cm². If the perimeter of the trapezium is 46 cm, what is the sum of the two non-parallel sides?
A parallelogram is made up of 2 identical triangles. Each triangle has a base of 16 cm and a height of 9 cm. Find the area of the parallelogram.
The length of a rectangle is 6 3/4 cm. The width is 4/9 of its length. What is the area of the rectangle?
The base of a parallelogram is 24.5 cm. Its height is 0.4 times its base. Find its area.
The area of a parallelogram is 195 m². Find the base of the parallelogram if the height is 15 m.
How many 4-cm squares can be cut from a piece of paper 25 cm long and 18 cm wide at most?
The side length of a square is 7.2 cm. What is its area?
The upper base and lower base of a trapezium are 12 cm and 20 cm respectively. The area is 96 cm². Find the height of the trapezium.
An isosceles triangle has two equal sides of 13 cm each and a base of 10 cm. The height from the apex to the base is 12 cm. Find the area of the triangle.
A right-angled triangle has a base of 12 cm and a height of 7 cm. Find its area.
Which of the following 3-D shapes has a triangular face?
Name the 3-D shape which has 2 identical circular faces and 1 curved surface.
The length, width and height of a cuboid are 18 cm, 8 cm and 5 cm respectively. It is cut into 6 identical cuboids. What is the volume of each small cuboid?
Name the 3-D shape which has a rectangular base and 4 triangular faces meeting at a point.
A box is 2 1/4 m long and 4/5 m wide. Its volume is 2 7/10 m³. Find its height.
A rectangular box is 24 cm long, 12 cm wide and 8 cm high. How many 4-cm cubes can be put in the box at most?
Denise cuts out the largest cube from a cuboid that is 15 cm long, 6 cm wide and 4 cm tall. Find the volume of the remaining part of the cuboid.
Which of the following 3-D shapes has a different number of vertices from the others?
A 3-D shape has 5 faces, 5 vertices and 8 edges. Name the 3-D shape.
The volume of a rectangular container is 270 cm³. Its length is 10 cm and its width is 4.5 cm. Find its height.
RS is the radius of Circle P. If RS is x cm long, find the circumference of Circle P in terms of x. (Take π = 3.14)
P is the centre of the circle. The perimeter of triangle DEF is 12 cm. One side DE = 4 cm and EF = 2.4 cm. Find the radius of the circle.
Which of the following statements about circles is CORRECT?
options: A. The diameter is shorter than the radius
B. Two radii of the same circle can have different lengths
C. The circumference of a circle is always larger than its diameter
D. The centre of a circle is on the circumference

text: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: A. A square has only 1 line of symmetry
B. A rhombus has 4 right angles
C. A rectangle has 2 pairs of equal opposite sides
D. A parallelogram has 4 equal sides

text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: A. A square has 4 equal sides
B. A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides
C. A trapezoid has 2 pairs of parallel sides
D. A rhombus has 4 equal sides

text: A parallelogram has sides of 5 cm and 8 cm. When it is divided along a diagonal, which of the following is correct?
options: A. Two equilateral triangles are formed
B. Two right-angled triangles are formed
C. Two identical scalene triangles are formed
D. Two isosceles triangles are formed

text: Which of the following unit conversions is correct?
Amy ran 2 1/3 laps on Monday, 2 5/6 laps on Tuesday and 1 7/12 laps on Wednesday. Which of the following statements is correct?
options: A. Amy ran the most laps on Wednesday
B. Amy ran 5 1/6 laps on Monday and Tuesday altogether
C. Amy ran more than 7 laps on these three days in total
D. Amy ran more than 2 laps on Wednesday

text: On a map, the School is directly north of the Park. The Library is directly west of the School. What is the direction of the Library from the Park?
On a map, each grid square represents 500 m × 500 m. Alice walks directly east from point A to point B, covering 3 grid squares. How far does Alice walk?
